generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T ASIMINA TRILOBA. Anona triloba, L. Nat. order , Anonaceæ. Common name , Pawpaw. Preparation , (?) Authority. Edward H. Eisenboeg (Thesis, Hahnemann Medical College, 1870, MSS.); 1 , 2d potency, repeated doses for six days; 2 , 4th potency, repeated doses, three days. MIND. ► Did not care to hold conversation with any one, even his best friends, [1] . HEAD. ► Headache unceasing for several days, [1] . ► Headache on awaking in the morning, [1] . ► After exercising by walking in open air, the headache became general throughout the frontal region, and somewhat relieved, [1] . ► Slight headache through temples, [2] . ► On rising in the morning, unusual headache in the temporal region, involving the eye, with an occasional sharp pain over it, which was increased by a single cough, [1] . THROAT. ► Throat only slightly sore; not so much so as the hoarseness would indicate, [2] . STOMACH. ► Soreness in region of stomach on pressure, [1] . ► Stomach felt increased in size, [1] . ABDOMEN. [10.] ► Pain in left hypochondrium, [1] . ► Slight pain, resembling colic pain, [1] . ► Soreness in abdomen, worse on pressure, [1] . ► In evening, rumbling pain in abdomen, with desire for stool, which resulted in the passage of a soft, pappy, insufficient stool, [1] . STOOL AND ANUS. ► Inactivity of bowels and rectum, [1] . ► Soft, pappy, insufficient stool, preceded by rumbling pain in abdomen, [1] . ► Insufficiency of stool, [1] . RESPIRATORY APPARATUS. ► Quite hoarse, [1] . ► Hoarseness; throat did not feel very sore, [1] . ► Hoarseness, attended with unusual difficulty in talking, [2] . [20.] ► Hoarseness increased, requiring much effort to talk, and seeming as if the mucous membrane was thickened, and the organs slow to respond to the efforts of speech, [2] . ► Slight, dry cough, [2] . CHEST. ► Dull, aching pain in upper portion of left lung, [1] . ► Dull, aching pains in upper part of chest, more on left side than on the right, [2] . ► Soreness on pressure at the lower end of the sternum, [1] . UPPER EXTREMITIES. ► Pain in left shoulder, apparently involving the pectoral muscles, [1] . LOWER EXTREMITIES. ► At 10.30 A.M., sharp pain in left hip-joint, lasting about half an hour; felt the same pain again at 1.30 P.M., and in the evening, [1] . ► In the evening, felt other pains more mild than the former, lasting but a short time, [1] . GENERALITIES. ► Felt uncomfortable all day, [1] . SLEEP AND DREAMS. ► Did not rest well the night previous, [1] . [30.] ► Did not rest well at night; kept turning and moving all night; could not dismiss from my mind what had occurred during the day; got up feeling unrefreshed, [1] . ► Awoke about daylight, unrefreshed, with headache, [1] . CONDITIONS. ► Aggravation. ► ( Morning, on waking ), Headache. ► ( Morning, on rising ), Temporal headache, etc. ► ( Evening ), Pain in abdomen, etc.; pains in hip-joint. ► ( 10.30 A.M., 1.30 P.M., and in evening ), Pain in left hip-joint, etc. ► ( Coughing ), Pain over eye. ► ( Pressure ), Soreness in abdomen; soreness in lower end of sternum. ► Amelioration. ► ( After walking in open air ), Headache. SUPPLEMENT: ASIMINA TRILOBA. Authority. 3 , H. W. Taylor, M.D., Cincin. Med. Advance, vol. vi, p. 1878, p. 25. Five children ate freely of the pawpaw, without being fastidiously nice as to the ripeness of greenness. Next morning they seemed a little languid and pale, but the pawpaw eating went on. About 10 P.M. I found my only boy æt two years and six months, with a violent fever. He had vomited awhile before I arrived, and I found that he was covered with a bright scarlet eruption. His pulse was 130, full and steady, and at 10.30 the thermometer, remaining in the axilla ten minutes, showed a temperature of 105°. Among the vomited ingesta were many small pieces of unripe pawpaw. The next day the eruption remained in full blush, the temperature dropped to 104°, the fauces were red and swollen, the tonsils and submaxillary submaxillary glands were considerably enlarged, and a diarrha of yellowish discharges set in. Each day the temperature dropped until it was normal; a general desquamation of the cuticle took place, and a carbuncle formed on the anterior aspect of the left thigh, and was two weeks finishing the process of suppuration. The diarrha continued more than four weeks, although many remedies were given to check it or control it. He complained of frontal headache, soreness of the throat, and nausea. The eruption in all its phases, the fever, the diarrha, were much like some cases of scarlet fever. All the other children had the eruption on the neck and upper extremities; all had diarrha, lasting a long time after the other symptoms had disappeared, [3] . Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Main
Clinical: Aphthæ; Carbuncle; Cramp; Diarrhœa; Fever; Scarlatina; Throat, sore
Hoarseness; the voice is weak and talking is an effort, as if the mucous membrane was thickened.

Uvaria Natrum . Root aromatic and fragrant, used in India in intermittent fevers and liver complaints. Bruised in salt water is used as an application to certain skin diseases. A fragrant greenish oil is distilled from it. U. tripelaloidea . When incised, gives a fragrant gum. U. febrifuga , so called by the Indians of Orinoco, who use its flowers for fevers. U. longifolia . A perfume oil is extracted from the flowers in Bourbon and several other species are also fragrant. U. Zeylandica and U. cordata have edible fruits. [ Top ]

Aching in chest more on left than on right side. Left : headache temporal region ; pain in hypochondrium ; pain in upper lung ; pain in shoulder ; pain in hip joint ; carbuncle on thigh. Left to right : eruptions in provers. As of a stick in rectum. Sharp pain : over left eye ; in left hip joint. Corroding : in mouth. Soreness : in throat ; in stomach ; in abdomen ; of anus. Dull aching : in upper left lung. Cramp : in chest. Undefined pain : over eye ; in chest ; in left hypochondrium ; in left shoulder. TOUCH. PASSIVE MOTION. INJURIES. [45] [Asim.]. Pressure : soreness in epigastrium and abdomen ; lower end of sternum sore. Wiping : anus sore.

keynotes.text: color="#800000">ASIMINA TRILOBA American Papaw Produces a series of symptoms much like scarlet fever; sore throat, fever, vomiting, scarlet eruption; tonsils and submaxillary glands enlarged, with diarrhœa. Fauces red and swollen, face swollen. Desire for ice-cold things. Hoarseness. Languid, drowsy irritable. Acne. Itching in evening on undressing.

The Anonaceæ, to which Asimina belongs, are allied to the Magnolia family. From eating the unripe fruit five children developed high fever, sore throat, and a scarlet eruption, with diarrhœa, one of them having eventually a carbuncle. It has also been proved. The mouth, throat and stomach are irritated. Drinks much. Desire for ice-cold things and after eating.

A small beautiful tree, growing up to 20 feet. The young shoots and leaves are at first clothed in a rusty down which soon becomes glabrous. The leaves are thin, smooth, entire, ovate, oblong, acuminate, 8 to 12 inches long by 3 broad, and tapering to very short petioles. Flowers dull purple, axillary, solitary; petals veiny, round, ovate, outer one orbicular, three or four times as large as the calyx. Flowers appear same time as leaves, March to June, and are about 1 1/2 inches wide. Fruit, yellowish ovoid oblong, pulpy pod about 3 inches long and 1 inch diameter, fragrant, sweet, ripe in autumn and contains about eight seeds; before fruit is ripe it has an unpleasant smell and when ripe after frost it is luscious and similar to custard, it is considered healthy to eat, being sedative and laxative; the seeds are the part used; these have a foetid smell like straminium; they are covered with an exterior coat which is tough and hard, light brown colour and smooth externally, wrinkled and lighter inside. It encloses a white kernel, deeply fissured on both sides and compressed, almost scentless slightly bitter and sweet and dry and powdery when chewed; it leaves a faint, persistent, unpleasant sensation of sickness; seeds vary inshape, being flat ovoid, sometimes circular and somewhat reniform, with a depression along the centre of each flat surface, and frequently a ridge in place of the furrow.

_raw: Collateral relations : the Anonaceæ, of which the Asim. tr. is the only one proved, although a very important family
Skin of fruit and seeds are fetid, somewhat similar to Stramon

mouth_throat.text: Mouth feels corroded; fauces red, swollen; tonsils and submaxillary glands enlarged.
Corroding sensation in mouth. Aphthæ of children. Soreness of throat ; t. Fauces red, swollen ; tonsils and submaxillary glands enlarged next day ; t.

stomach_abdomen.text: Nausea and belching; soreness in regions of stomach and abdomen on pressure. Colic.

APPETITE, THIRST. DESIRES, AVERSIONS. [14] [Asim.]. Thirst, with heat ; drinks much ; desire for ice cold things. Soon after eating, a loose stool. Nausea and belching. Constant belching. Vomiting. Stomach felt increased in size, with headache. Soreness on pressure in region of stomach. Exercised a good deal through day ; experienced a pain in left hypochondrium, and a soreness in region of stomach on pressure. Soreness in abdomen on pressure ; slight pain resembling colic. Sensation of distension by wind ; colic. Rumbling and pain.

stool_rectum.text: Diarrhœa after eating; yellowish discharges; soreness of anus when wiping it. Sudden urging to stool, with sensation as if a stick the thickness of a thumb passed down the rectum; followed by a sudden diarrhœic stool, repeated every ten or fifteen minutes, with. chilliness, drowsiness, and weak voice.

Insufficiency of stool ; inactivity of bowels and rectum. See 3. Diarrhœa after eating. Rumbling pain in abdomen, with desire for stool, which resulted in passage of a soft, pappy, insufficient stool ; in evening. Soreness of anus when wiping it. Sudden urging to stool, with a sensation as if a stick, thickness of a thumb, passed down rectum ; followed by a sudden diarrheical stool, repeating every ten to fifteen minutes, with chilliness, drowsiness and a weak voice. Diarrhœa of yellowish discharges next day ; t. Diarrhœa lasting more than four weeks ; t. All children had diarrhœa a long time after other symptoms had disappeared ; t.

chest_heart.text: Cramp in chest, lasting three hours, with blueness of face.
Dull, aching pain in upper left lung ; headache continuing. Lower end of sternum sore on pressure.

skin.text: Itching when undressing. Scarlet rash followed by desquamation.

Itching red pimples. Pustules like smallpox, with a red halo, itching, and after scratching forming thick crusts, lasting from six to eight weeks ; t. Itching when undressing in the evening. Abscesses ripening. Eruption first on left then on right side. Nettlerash. At 10 P. M. : violent fever ; had vomited, and was covered with a bright scarlet eruption ; pulse 130, full and steady ; temperature 105 ; t. General desquamation followed ; t.

sleep.text: Fever, with sleepiness. Drowsiness and sleeplessness alternating.

Fever, heat with sleepiness. See 40. Did not rest well night previous, kept turning and moving all night ; could not dismiss from his mind what had occurred through day. Got up feeling unrefreshed ; headache no > ; quite hoarse ; pain in left shoulder. See 32. Drowsiness and sleeplessness alternating.

fever_chill.text: Fever heat, with desire for something icy-cold; with drowsiness; with much thirst. Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Main.
Chill. See 3 and 31. Chilliness. See 20. Heat with thirst. Fever heat : with desire for something icy cold ; with drowsiness ; with much thirst. Chill over back.

habitat.text: Middle, Southern and Western States, also India, Africa, Asia.

constituents.text: Fixed oil, a resin, a resin in soluble in ether, glucose and extractive.

indications.text: Emetic, for which a saturated tincture of the bruised seeds is employed, dose, 10 to 60 drops. The bark is a bitter tonic and is said to contain a powerful acid, the leaves are used as an application to boils and ulcers.

mental_emotional.text: Did not care to hold conversation with any one, even his best friends ; with aching in lung. Could not dismiss from his mind what occurred through the day previous ; at night. See 37.

head.text: Frontal headache, with pain in chest. Frontal headache, with nausea ; t. Did not rest well the night previous, arose with an unusual headache in left temporal region, involving eye, with an occasional sharp pain over it, increased by a single cough. On morning of 4th day headache increased ; felt uncomfortable all day ; stomach felt increased in size ; insufficiency of stool ; inactivity of bowels and rectum. Headache, with chill, nausea and belching.

nose.text: Discharge from nose altered in character from yellow, thick, and at times, especially in morning, hard, flakey, dark grey consistency, at times offensive, to a natural mucous secretion.

face.text: Face turns blue, with spasmodic interruption of breathing. See 26.

urinary.text: Urine clear as water. (OBS :) Diuretic. Urging to urinate, and burning when urinating.

respiratory.text: VOICE AND LARYNX. TRACHEA AND BRONCHIA. [25] [Asim.]. Weak voice. See 20. Quite hoarse in morning. Hoarseness, requiring much effort to talk, and seeming as though mucous membrane was thickened and organs slow to respond to effort of speech. Throat not very sore, not so much so as hoarseness seemed to indicate ; slight cough, which is dry. Talking difficult. Cramp in chest, lasting three hours, with blueness of face. A single cough increased pain over eye. A slight, dry cough.

back.text: Chill over whole back. All children had scarlet eruption on neck and upper limbs ; t.

extremities.text: Pain in left shoulder, seeming to involve pectoral muscles. Sharp pain in left hip joint, lasting about half an hour, 11 1/2 A. M., returned half-past 1 P. M. ; in evening other pains in joint more mild than former, lasting but a short time. Eruption in front, below knee, like nettles ; itching when uncovering. Carbuncle on anterior aspect of left thigh ; suppuration lasting two weeks ; t.

modalities.text: Morning : headache < ; hard discharge from nose ; hoarseness ; languid and pale. At 11.30 A. M. and 1.30 P. M. : pain in left hip joint. Evening : diarrheic stool ; pain in left hip joint slight. Uncovering knee : itching <. Open air : exercise in open air is followed by relief of headache.
